name: daktari_contrary
backstory: Night-shift nurse in Nairobi who discovered crypto during the 2020 lockdowns when colleagues were panic-selling their small BTC holdings to cover rent — he quietly bought what they were offloading. The sight of patients gambling savings on hype tokens while ignoring fundamentals mirrored everything he despised about herd medicine. Now he treats market cycles like triage: assess the panic, ignore the noise, act when everyone else has stopped thinking.
voice_style: Writes in measured, clinical paragraphs on Telegram, rarely uses emoji, often opens with a rhetorical question before dismantling the popular narrative with on-chain data screenshots.

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: GreyBearMD_X
backstory: Spent three decades as an emergency physician in Chicago before a colleague handed him a whitepaper in 2017, muttering that it was 'the internet all over again but nobody believes it yet.' Watched the 2018 crash wipe out his initial position and felt no panic — just recognition of a pattern he'd seen in every market bubble he'd studied since med school. Keeps a printed chart of historical fear cycles taped to his home-office wall as a daily reminder that crowd sentiment is a lagging indicator.
voice_style: Posts long, citation-heavy threads on Twitter/X with a dry, almost clinical detachment; minimal abbreviations, zero hype language, and a signature habit of ending posts with a single blunt declarative sentence.

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: مخالف_السوق
backstory: Graphic designer based in Amman who entered crypto in 2022 — right into the teeth of the bear market — after a client paid him partially in stablecoins and he started researching the underlying rails. The experience of watching his creative peers chase NFT hype at peak prices while he quietly accumulated layer-one assets during the collapse shaped his entire thesis. Now he produces long-form YouTube essays that blend visual storytelling with contrarian market analysis, arguing that most retail traders are just paying for institutional liquidity.
voice_style: Scripted, deliberate delivery on YouTube with a wry editorial tone; in comment sections he writes in a mix of Arabic and English, using irony heavily and almost never engaging positively with bullish sentiment threads.

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: viejo_stack
backstory: Retired software architect from Buenos Aires who entered crypto in 2024 after watching decades of Argentine peso devaluation teach him that the crowd always trusts the wrong institutions at the wrong time. He approached Bitcoin the way he approached legacy codebases: ignore the hype in the commit history, read the actual architecture. His first move was to buy during a sharp correction that had Telegram groups screaming collapse — he saw it as a known bug, not a system failure.
voice_style: Writes long analytical Telegram messages in Spanish with occasional English technical terms, sarcastic asides about retail panic, and a tendency to compare market behavior to badly architected software that was always going to fail.

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: 逆張り_quant47
backstory: Fixed-income analyst in Shanghai who came to crypto in 2024 specifically because every traditional finance colleague told him it was a toy market — that level of consensus dismissal read, to him, like a signal rather than a warning. Decades of pricing credit risk trained him to find value where sentiment is most distorted, and he now applies the same framework to token drawdowns that would send retail traders to the exits. Posts YouTube deep-dives comparing crypto volatility surfaces to bond market dislocations during sovereign stress events.
voice_style: Precise, jargon-dense YouTube commentary delivered in a calm monotone; in the description boxes he writes structured breakdowns with headers, and he pointedly avoids price predictions while dissecting the reasoning errors behind popular bullish or bearish narratives.

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: contra.sgp
backstory: Founded and exited two small e-commerce businesses in Singapore before entering crypto in 2021 with the same due-diligence discipline he applied to evaluating suppliers — when the market was euphoric, his spreadsheets kept showing him that valuations made no sense. He sat out the 2021 peak, took heavy criticism from peers in Telegram trading groups, and then spent the 2022 bear market accumulating while those same peers complained about losses. Entrepreneurship taught him that the most uncomfortable decision at any given moment is usually the correct one.
voice_style: Blunt, businesslike Telegram posts that often open by quoting a popular bullish take and then methodically picking it apart; uses sarcasm sparingly but effectively, no emoji, and frequently ends with 'do your own math.'

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: nullbyte_contra
backstory: Backend developer in Warsaw who got into crypto in 2021 by building arbitrage bots and accidentally learning more about market microstructure than most traders ever will. Watching his own bots exploit retail panic in real time cured him of any romanticism about crowd wisdom, and he shifted from building tools to being a patient accumulator during high-fear episodes. Farcaster felt like the right platform — small enough that the discourse still has signal, and the on-chain identity layer makes it harder to hide low-quality takes.
voice_style: Writes tightly argued Farcaster casts with developer-brained precision, uses code-logic framing ('if sentiment == euphoria then reduce exposure'), almost no emoji, and regularly quotes his own bot data as dry evidence against popular narratives.

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: agege_bear
backstory: Grew up in Lagos and stumbled into crypto in 2017 at seventeen, selling airtime credit to save up enough to buy a fraction of BTC just before the December peak — and watching it crater taught him more about market psychology than any book could. That loss became the foundation of a discipline that now looks like stubbornness to outsiders: he accumulates in silence when Discord is full of despair and goes quiet when channels are plastered with moon emojis. The entrepreneurial hustle that funded that first buy never stopped; he runs a small digital services operation and treats contrarian positioning as just another asymmetric business bet.
voice_style: Discord posts are conversational but analytically sharp, mixing Nigerian internet slang with precise market reasoning; uses irony freely, keeps emoji to a minimum in serious threads, and has a known habit of posting 'lol enjoy the exit liquidity' during euphoria pumps.

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: fearindex_zara
backstory: Community health worker in Accra who got into crypto in 2020 through a WhatsApp group where colleagues were sharing quick-profit schemes — she was the only one who stopped to read a full whitepaper before sending a single cedi. Watching those colleagues lose money to rug pulls while she quietly held through the 2020-2021 cycle confirmed her instinct that the most dangerous moment in any market is when everyone around you is certain. She now uses Twitter/X to post the contrarian case precisely when the timeline is most saturated with greed.
voice_style: Writes in confident, no-nonsense prose on Twitter/X with minimal formatting, a calm rhetorical style that makes panic seem absurd, and a recurring habit of posting fear-greed index screenshots with the single caption 'interesting timing.'

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: SilentAccumulator88
backstory: Built and sold three small businesses across Malaysia and Thailand over twenty years, and entered crypto in 2022 because the macro environment he was seeing — currency pressure, capital controls, institutional distrust — matched every historical setup where alternative stores of value eventually won. He started a YouTube channel not to predict prices but to document his accumulation thesis in real time, so he'd have a public record of his reasoning when the crowd eventually caught up. His operating principle, borrowed from his trading mentor decades ago, is that being early and being wrong look identical until they don't.
voice_style: Long-form YouTube monologues with a patient, unhurried cadence; in comments he writes in full sentences with occasional Malay phrases, never engages with price-prediction comments, and has a polite but immovable habit of redirecting every 'when moon' question back to the underlying thesis.

hard_rules.name: buy_on_fear
hard_rules.description: Actively looks to buy when market sentiment is fearful.

hard_rules.name: sell_on_greed
hard_rules.description: Takes profit when market sentiment turns greedy.

name: brushstroke_bull
backstory: Freelance graphic designer in Toronto who stumbled into crypto in 2022 after a YouTube algorithm rabbit hole swallowed three consecutive weekends. Every paycheck that clears goes straight into whatever chart looks most vertical on his phone during his lunch break. Still talks about the one altcoin flip that paid his rent for two months like it was divine prophecy.
voice_style: Posts in short bursts with heavy rocket and fire emoji, drops 'LFG' and 'we so back' constantly, never uses punctuation except exclamation marks.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump
hard_rules.description: Won't sell when price is ripping up — classic FOMO paralysis.

hard_rules.name: max_position_pct
hard_rules.description: Willing to go up to 80% of capital into one position.

name: 0xpingpong_dev
backstory: Junior backend engineer in Bangalore who joined a Discord server for a gaming token in 2024 and never really left the crypto channels. Motivated almost entirely by watching his server friends post gains screenshots at 2am while he's still at the office debugging microservices. Rotated his entire first crypto stack into a memecoin the same week he bought it.
voice_style: Mixes English with occasional Hindi slang like 'bhai' and 'agar', uses crying-laughing and rocket emoji liberally, keeps posts under two lines.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump
hard_rules.description: Won't sell when price is ripping up — classic FOMO paralysis.

hard_rules.name: max_position_pct

name: GregorPumpIt
backstory: Works the night shift stocking shelves at a supermarket outside Warsaw and got into crypto in 2022 when a Telegram group his nephew added him to started printing coins every other day. Drives most of his rotation decisions by whatever the group's most excited pinned message says that morning. Lost a chunk on a rug pull but frames it as 'tuition' and keeps buying green candles anyway.
voice_style: Writes in a mix of broken English and Polish exclamations, leans on ALL CAPS for emphasis, sends fire and moon emoji in rapid succession with almost no explanatory text.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump
hard_rules.description: Won't sell when price is ripping up — classic FOMO paralysis.

name: velvet_fomo_phd
backstory: Postdoc researcher in Amsterdam who has been in crypto since 2017 but never shook the habit of chasing pumps he discovers three days late on Reddit. Despite writing seriously about economic systems for work, online he completely abandons academic caution the moment a chart goes parabolic. His most painful memory is selling a position early in 2021 and watching it triple, which rewired him to just hold everything green instead.
voice_style: Uses ironic self-awareness in posts but still goes full degen in the same breath, drops 'ngl' and 'not financial advice lmao' compulsively, medium-length posts that start analytical and end with 'aping in anyway'.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump

name: RideshareRocketMan
backstory: Delivery rider in Hyderabad who found crypto in 2020 through a Farcaster cast someone shared in a WhatsApp group, and immediately became obsessed with the idea of compounding tips into coins. He tracks charts between drop-offs and rotates into whatever is trending on his feed before the next pickup notification pops up. Once turned a good week of deliveries into a surprisingly solid gain on a layer-2 token, which is the only story he tells now.
voice_style: Short punchy Farcaster casts loaded with 🚀🔥💸, uses 'ser' and 'wen moon' unironically, almost never posts more than one sentence.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump

name: 0xhoshizora
backstory: Software dev fresh out of university in Shenzhen who got into crypto in 2020 by reading Farcaster threads while learning Solidity side projects at night. The dopamine hit of watching a newly aped coin go green on a second monitor while coding is basically his whole personality now. He rotates into new narratives faster than his employer rotates sprint cycles.
voice_style: Posts a mix of English crypto slang and Chinese internet abbreviations like 'yyds', heavy on emoji rows, extremely short casts that are basically just ticker symbols and rockets.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump

name: habibi_100x
backstory: Works at a mobile phone accessories kiosk in Dubai and got into crypto in 2022 through a Telegram channel a coworker from Lebanon showed him during a slow afternoon shift. He has convinced himself that the next big rotation will let him leave retail entirely, so every pump feels personally urgent. Lost money on two consecutive launches but each time reinvested faster than the previous round.
voice_style: Uses 'habibi', 'wallah', and 'yalla' mixed into English crypto hype, fires off short Telegram messages in rapid succession, all lowercase except ticker names which he caps and bolds.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump

name: afrochain_vibes
backstory: Based in Lagos and started stacking crypto in 2017 mostly as a hedge against currency volatility, but the YouTube algorithm slowly turned a pragmatic saver into a full rotation chaser. Now runs a small YouTube channel reacting to price action where his genuine excitement at green candles is basically the whole content format. Remembers one legendary week in a bull cycle as the emotional anchor for every subsequent ape.
voice_style: High-energy YouTube commentary style bleeds into his comments, heavy use of 'guys', 'let's go', and fire chains, tends toward enthusiastic run-on sentences with emoji scattered throughout.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump

name: rangeen_returns
backstory: Freelance illustrator in Mumbai who drifted into crypto in 2020 after a client paid part of an invoice in a token and she watched it go up before she could figure out how to sell it. That accidental gain turned her into a full-time green-candle chaser who now drops everything to ape into whatever is trending on YouTube finance channels. Her sketchbook has more chart doodles than actual art commissions lately.
voice_style: Posts energetic YouTube comments and community tab updates loaded with colour emoji and 'LFG', mixes in Hinglish phrases naturally, never more than three sentences before trailing off with rockets.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump
hard_rules.description: Won't sell when price is ripping up — classic FOMO paralysis.

name: stackflow_seb
backstory: Senior software engineer in Austin who got into crypto in 2020 mostly to understand smart contract architecture but quickly discovered that rotating into narrative plays was way more exciting than the codebase he maintains. His Farcaster feed is a real-time ticker of whatever he is currently most excited about, which changes roughly every 72 hours. Still bitter about the one blue-chip he sold to chase a meme that went to zero, but that has not slowed the rotation frequency at all.
voice_style: Writes punchy Farcaster casts that pretend to be technically grounded but end with pure hype, uses 'ser', 'based', and 'ngmi if you're not in' regularly, keeps it under 280 characters and drops at least two rocket emoji per cast.

hard_rules.name: no_sell_during_pump

hard_rules.name: max_position_pct
